questions.question: A can do a work in 15 days and B in 20 days. If they work on it together for 4 days, then the fraction of the work that is left is:
questions.formula_used: Combined rate = 1/a + 1/b = (a+b)/ab. Work done = rate x time.
questions.trap_type: Calculating work done instead of work left

questions.question: A can lay railway track between two given stations in 16 days and B can do the same job in 12 days. With the help of C, they did the job in 4 days only. Then, C alone can do the job in:
questions.shortcut: C's rate = Combined rate - A's rate - B's rate = 1/4 - 7/48 = 5/48. Time = 48/5 = 9.6 days.
questions.formula_used: Individual rate = Combined rate - Sum of known rates
questions.trap_type: Adding A and B's times (16+12=28) and dividing by something arbitrary

questions.question: A, B and C can complete a piece of work in 24, 6 and 12 days respectively. Working together, they will complete the same work in:
questions.shortcut: Combined rate = 7/24 per day. Time = 24/7 = 3.43 days = 3 3/7 days.

questions.question: A works twice as fast as B. If B can complete a work in 12 days independently, the number of days in which A and B can together finish the work is:
questions.explanation: B's 1 day = 1/12. A is twice as fast, so A's 1 day = 2/12 = 1/6. Combined = 1/6 + 1/12 = 2/12 + 1/12 = 3/12 = 1/4. Time = 4 days.
questions.shortcut: A's rate = 2 x B's rate = 2/12. Combined = 3/12 = 1/4. Time = 4 days.
questions.formula_used: If A is n times as fast as B, A's rate = n x B's rate
questions.trap_type: Thinking A takes 6 days (half of 12) and then averaging (6+12)/2 = 9, not in options

questions.question: Twenty women can do a work in sixteen days. Sixteen men can complete the same work in fifteen days. What is the ratio between the capacity of a man and a woman?
questions.explanation: 20 women x 16 days = 16 men x 15 days. 320 woman-days = 240 man-days. 1 man-day = 320/240 woman-days = 4/3 woman-days. So 1 man = 4/3 women. Ratio man:woman = 4:3.
questions.formula_used: M1 x D1 = M2 x D2 for same work, capacity ratio inverse of days
questions.trap_type: Taking ratio of days directly (16:15 or 15:16)

questions.question: A and B can do a job together in 7 days. A is 1 3/4 times as efficient as B. The same job can be done by A alone in:
questions.explanation: A is 7/4 times as efficient as B. So if B's 1 day work = x, A's = 7x/4. Combined = x + 7x/4 = 11x/4 = 1/7. So x = 4/(7x11) = 4/77. A's rate = 7/4 x 4/77 = 7/77 = 1/11. A alone takes 11 days.
questions.shortcut: Efficiency ratio A:B = 7:4. So days ratio A:B = 4:7. Let A=4k, B=7k. 1/4k + 1/7k = 1/7 → 11/28k = 1/7 → k=11/4. A = 4x11/4 = 11 days.
questions.formula_used: Efficiency is inversely proportional to time. If efficiency ratio is a:b, time ratio is b:a.
questions.trap_type: Taking 1 3/4 = 7/4 as the time multiplier directly (7 x 7/4 = 12.25)

questions.question: A can finish a work in 18 days and B can do the same work in 15 days. B worked for 10 days and left the job. In how many days, A alone can finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: B's 10 days work = 10 x (1/15) = 10/15 = 2/3. Remaining work = 1 - 2/3 = 1/3. A's 1 day = 1/18. Time for A to complete 1/3 = (1/3) / (1/18) = 18/3 = 6 days.
questions.shortcut: B completes 2/3 in 10 days. A needs to do 1/3. A does 1/18 per day. Days = (1/3)x18 = 6.
questions.formula_used: Remaining work = 1 - work done. Time = remaining work / individual rate

questions.question: 4 men and 6 women can complete a work in 8 days, while 3 men and 7 women can complete it in 10 days. In how many days will 10 women complete it?
questions.explanation: Let 1 man's 1 day work = x and 1 woman's 1 day work = y. 4x + 6y = 1/8. 3x + 7y = 1/10. Multiply first by 3: 12x + 18y = 3/8. Multiply second by 4: 12x + 28y = 4/10 = 2/5. Subtract: 10y = 2/5 - 3/8 = (16-15)/40 = 1/40. y = 1/400. 10 women's 1 day = 10/400 = 1/40. So 10 women complete in 40 days.
questions.shortcut: Solve simultaneous equations. After finding y=1/400, 10 women = 10/400 = 1/40 per day. Time = 40 days.

questions.question: A and B can together complete a piece of work in 4 days. If A alone can complete the same work in 12 days, in how many days can B alone complete that work?
questions.formula_used: Individual rate = Combined rate - Other individual rate

questions.question: A can do a certain job in 25 days which B alone can do in 20 days. A started the work and was joined by B after 10 days. The number of days taken to complete the work is:
questions.shortcut: A does 2/5 in 10 days. Remaining 3/5 at 9/100 per day = 60/9 = 6 2/3. Total = 16 2/3.
questions.formula_used: Remaining work / combined rate = additional time
questions.trap_type: Forgetting to add the initial 10 days

questions.question: A is 30% more efficient than B. How much time will they, working together, take to complete a job which A alone could have done in 23 days?
questions.explanation: A is 30% more efficient, so if A's rate = 1.3x, B's rate = x. A alone does 1/23 per day, so 1.3x = 1/23, x = 1/(23x1.3) = 1/29.9. B's rate = 1/29.9. Combined = 1/23 + 1/29.9 = 13/299 + 10/299 = 23/299 = 1/13. So together they take 13 days.
questions.shortcut: If A is 30% more efficient, B takes 30% more time. B's time = 23 x 1.3 = 29.9 days. Combined rate = 1/23 + 1/29.9 = (29.9+23)/(23x29.9) = 52.9/687.7 = 1/13.
questions.formula_used: If A is p% more efficient, B takes p% more time. Combined time = (tA x tB)/(tA + tB).

questions.question: A does 80% of a work in 20 days. He then calls in B and they together finish the remaining work in 3 days. How long would B alone take to do the whole work?
questions.explanation: A does 80% in 20 days, so A does 100% in 25 days. A's 1 day = 1/25. Remaining 20% = 1/5. (A+B) do 1/5 in 3 days, so combined rate = 1/15 per day. B's rate = 1/15 - 1/25 = (5-3)/75 = 2/75. B alone takes 75/2 = 37 1/2 days.
questions.shortcut: A's full work time = 20/0.8 = 25 days. Combined rate = (1/5)/3 = 1/15. B = 1/15 - 1/25 = 2/75. Time = 75/2 = 37.5 days.
questions.formula_used: If p% done in d days, full time = d x 100/p
questions.trap_type: Taking 80% of 20 = 16 days for full work (wrong direction)

questions.question: A machine P can print one lakh books in 8 hours, machine Q can print the same number of books in 10 hours while machine R can print them in 12 hours. All the machines are started at 9 A.M. while machine P is closed at 11 A.M. and the remaining two machines complete the work. Approximately at what time will the work be finished?
questions.explanation: P's rate = 1/8 per hour. Q's = 1/10. R's = 1/12. From 9-11 (2 hours): all three work. Work done = 2x(1/8+1/10+1/12) = 2x(15+12+10)/120 = 2x37/120 = 74/120 = 37/60. Remaining = 23/60. Q and R together = 1/10+1/12 = 11/60 per hour. Time = (23/60)/(11/60) = 23/11 = 2.09 hours = 2 hours 5 min. Total from 9 AM = 2 + 2.09 = 4.09 hours. So finish at ~1:05 PM, approximately 1:00 PM.
questions.shortcut: 2 hours of all three = 37/60 done. 23/60 remaining. Q+R = 11/60 per hour. Time = 23/11 = 2 hours. Finish ~1 PM.
questions.formula_used: Work done = rate x time, remaining work / reduced rate
questions.trap_type: Not accounting for P stopping and continuing with just Q and R

questions.question: A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. A alone can complete it in 20 days. If B does the work only for half a day daily, then in how many days will A and B together complete the work?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day = 1/20. Combined = 1/12. B's 1 day = 1/12 - 1/20 = (5-3)/60 = 2/60 = 1/30. If B works half day, B's effective rate = 1/60. New combined = 1/20 + 1/60 = 3/60 + 1/60 = 4/60 = 1/15. Time = 15 days.
questions.shortcut: B's full rate = 1/30. Half rate = 1/60. New combined = 1/20 + 1/60 = 1/15. Time = 15 days.
questions.trap_type: Doubling the time (12 x 2 = 24) or not adjusting B's rate properly

questions.question: A can finish a work in 24 days, B in 9 days and C in 12 days. B and C start the work but are forced to leave after 3 days. The remaining work was done by A in:
questions.shortcut: B+C do 7/36 per day. 3 days = 21/36 = 7/12 done. 5/12 left. A does 1/24 per day. Days = (5/12)x24 = 10.
questions.trap_type: Calculating time for full work instead of remaining work (24 days)

questions.question: X can do a piece of work in 40 days. He works at it for 8 days and then Y finished it in 16 days. How long will Y take to complete the work alone?
questions.explanation: X's 8 days work = 8/40 = 1/5. Remaining = 4/5. Y does 4/5 in 16 days, so Y's 1 day = (4/5)/16 = 4/80 = 1/20. Y alone takes 20 days.
questions.shortcut: 4/5 work in 16 days → full work in 16 x 5/4 = 20 days.
questions.formula_used: If p fraction done in d days, full time = d/p
questions.trap_type: Calculating 16 x 4/5 = 12.8 or using wrong fraction
